posts.text:
The West is relatively “protected" from the current energy shock:

Oil currently flowing through global shipping routes is down to ~1,430 million barrels, down -270 million over the last 3 weeks.

Meanwhile, OECD Europe and Americas commercial crude flows are up to ~960 million barrels, the highest since at least 2024.

Since the start of 2026, Western commercial crude flows have risen +50 million barrels, even as global oil in transit collapses.

In fact, US oil giants are now set for one of their most profitable years on record.

The US is pumping and selling oil like never before.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text:
Was just thinking about the Internet upgrades that will come from Turbostratic Fractal Graphene and the power savings. Ai summary...

"True value creation means upgrading the physical foundation of our world. Integrating Hydrograph’s 99.8% pure graphene into the internet's backbone fundamentally changes the math of global infrastructure.

By replacing legacy materials, it unlocks femtosecond data switching for 100x faster speeds, creates near-indestructible submarine cables, and acts as a massive thermal heat sink for data centers.

This single material upgrade could slash the internet's energy consumption by up to 50%, saving hundreds of terawatt-hours globally. We aren't just tweaking existing technology; we are engineering a hyper-efficient, highly scalable future from the atomic level up."

Thats equates to a couple percent of global power usage... just crazy to think.
posts.author: BambroughKevin
